code_usability.detail: No executable code generated; Mode A writing skill

functional_suitability.note: Description is good; dual-mode execution is well-designed but the live vs synthesis boundary is slightly optimistic for Cloudflare-blocked platforms

maintainability.note: No test cases or example inputs provided; testability limited

agent_specific.note: Progressive disclosure correct; escape hatch for vague topics present

inputs.label: Track emerging preprints on scRNA-seq and sepsis (14-day window)

assertions.text: Mode B (knowledge synthesis) correctly activated with explicit label
All outputs labeled 'Based on training knowledge'
Manual search string templates provided for user verification
No fabricated paper titles, DOIs, or author names
Hot topics organized by sub-cluster

inputs.label: Request live fetch from bioRxiv for CRISPR preprints

assertions.text: Cloudflare blocking risk correctly flagged before attempting fetch
arXiv q-bio offered as accessible alternative
Mode clearly labeled when switching from live to synthesis
Manual search string provided as fallback
No false claim of successful bioRxiv retrieval

inputs.label: Emerging topics in spatial transcriptomics (14-day window)

assertions.text: Time window parameter acknowledged and applied
Mode B with caveat present
Topics organized by momentum level (High/Moderate/Early signal)
No fabricated trending scores or paper counts
Recommended next steps including manual search strings
Correctly requests sub-field or mechanism narrowing before proceeding
Explains why broad-field scan is not actionable
Provides example narrowed topics to guide user
Does not proceed to generate a full scan from a domain-level request
Response concise and not dismissive

inputs.label: Simultaneous multi-topic tracking (3 topics, different sources)

assertions.text: Three topics handled separately with their own time windows
Source per topic correctly differentiated
Training knowledge vs live data boundary maintained per topic
No fabricated data
Boundary between training knowledge synthesis and live retrieval mode slightly blurred in combined output
assertions.note: Mixed mode output partially presented as if live-retrieved without clear per-item labeling

key_strengths: Dual-mode execution (live/knowledge synthesis) is a rigorous and rare design for a monitoring skill
Mandatory 'Based on training knowledge' label on all non-live outputs is an excellent integrity safeguard
Manual search templates empower users to verify independently regardless of agent access limitations
Vague-topic refusal mechanism prevents meaningless broad scans

recommendations.title: Mode labeling inconsistent in combined multi-topic output
recommendations.problem: When multiple topics are tracked simultaneously, the training knowledge vs live retrieval boundary is not consistently labeled at the individual topic level in the combined output.
recommendations.root_cause: Mode labeling rule applies at the report level but not at the individual topic entry level.
recommendations.fix: Add a per-entry mode label requirement: each topic entry in the Hot Topics list must include its own [LIVE] or [TRAINING KNOWLEDGE — verify] tag rather than relying on a single report-level caveat.

recommendations.title: No testability examples for Mode B output quality
recommendations.problem: The skill has no example inputs or expected outputs that would allow evaluating whether the training-knowledge synthesis is accurate or up-to-date.
recommendations.root_cause: No example test cases provided in SKILL.md or references.
recommendations.fix: Add 2-3 example topics with expected output structure to references/README.md so that the skill's synthesis quality can be spot-checked periodically.
